entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note:
Initial autoresearch harness with naive O(N²) schoolbook negacyclic polynomial
multiplication in `Z[X]/(X^1024+1)` with `u32` wrapping coefficients. `Plan` is a
placeholder; all work happens in `poly_mul`.
entries.approach:
Initial autoresearch harness with naive O(N²) schoolbook negacyclic polynomial
multiplication in `Z[X]/(X^1024+1)` with `u32` wrapping coefficients. `Plan` is a
placeholder; all work happens in `poly_mul`.
entries.evalSnapshot: SCORE: 319558256 (deterministic wasm WORK; lower is better)

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note:
Replace naive O(N^2) schoolbook with an O(N log N) negacyclic NTT.
Three NTT-friendly primes (998244353, 1004535809, 985661441), each with 2048 | (p-1) and primitive root 3, carry a length-1024 negacyclic transform via psi pre/post weighting (psi a primitive 2N-th root). Forward transform is decimation-in-frequency and the inverse is decimation-in-time, so no bit-reversal permutation is needed. Per-prime residue products are recombined with Garner CRT; since |signed product coefficient| < 2^74 and P0*P1*P2 ~= 2^89.7, the exact integer is recovered and reduced mod 2^32 (sign decided by the top mixed-radix digit, no u128 in the hot path).
All twiddle/psi/CRT tables are precomputed in plan_new (free under the differential fuel metric). Hot loops keep residues < 2^32 so reductions are single u64 % ops, and use get_unchecked to drop bounds checks.
entries.approach:
Replace naive O(N^2) schoolbook with an O(N log N) negacyclic NTT.
Three NTT-friendly primes (998244353, 1004535809, 985661441), each with 2048 | (p-1) and primitive root 3, carry a length-1024 negacyclic transform via psi pre/post weighting (psi a primitive 2N-th root). Forward transform is decimation-in-frequency and the inverse is decimation-in-time, so no bit-reversal permutation is needed. Per-prime residue products are recombined with Garner CRT; since |signed product coefficient| < 2^74 and P0*P1*P2 ~= 2^89.7, the exact integer is recovered and reduced mod 2^32 (sign decided by the top mixed-radix digit, no u128 in the hot path).
All twiddle/psi/CRT tables are precomputed in plan_new (free under the differential fuel metric). Hot loops keep residues < 2^32 so reductions are single u64 % ops, and use get_unchecked to drop bounds checks.
entries.iterationNotes: Local SCORE 51690192 (6.18x vs baseline 319558256). All 7 correctness tests pass; only src/algorithm/ changed.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Transform both multiply operands (a and b) in a single lockstep forward NTT (ntt_dif2) so the loop control, index arithmetic (lo/hi), and twiddle load are computed once and reused for both butterflies instead of twice. Pure overhead reduction; algorithm and outputs are unchanged. ~5.9% fewer fuel operators.
entries.approach: Transform both multiply operands (a and b) in a single lockstep forward NTT (ntt_dif2) so the loop control, index arithmetic (lo/hi), and twiddle load are computed once and reused for both butterflies instead of twice. Pure overhead reduction; algorithm and outputs are unchanged. ~5.9% fewer fuel operators.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Combine each pair of consecutive radix-2 DIF (Gentleman-Sande) forward stages into a single fused radix-4 butterfly. This is exactly two radix-2 stages computed with the intermediate values kept in registers, so the butterfly network and the resulting bit-reversed ordering are unchanged (still compatible with the radix-2 DIT inverse) -- but the forward transform now makes 5 memory passes instead of 10, halving load/store traffic. Twiddles ta=w_{4q}^j, tb=ta*w_4, tc=ta^2 are precomputed per fused stage in plan_new (free). Both multiply operands stay fused in lockstep. ~12% fewer fuel operators.
entries.approach: Combine each pair of consecutive radix-2 DIF (Gentleman-Sande) forward stages into a single fused radix-4 butterfly. This is exactly two radix-2 stages computed with the intermediate values kept in registers, so the butterfly network and the resulting bit-reversed ordering are unchanged (still compatible with the radix-2 DIT inverse) -- but the forward transform now makes 5 memory passes instead of 10, halving load/store traffic. Twiddles ta=w_{4q}^j, tb=ta*w_4, tc=ta^2 are precomputed per fused stage in plan_new (free). Both multiply operands stay fused in lockstep. ~12% fewer fuel operators.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Mirror the radix-4 forward fusion on the inverse: combine each pair of consecutive radix-2 DIT (Cooley-Tukey) inverse stages into one fused radix-4 butterfly, keeping intermediates in registers. The butterfly network is unchanged (still the exact inverse of the forward, bit-reversed input -> natural output), but the inverse transform now makes 5 memory passes instead of 10. Inverse twiddles ita=w_{4q}^{-j}, itb=w_{4q}^{-(j+q)}, itc=w_{2q}^{-j} are precomputed per fused stage in plan_new (free). ~9.5% fewer fuel operators.
entries.approach: Mirror the radix-4 forward fusion on the inverse: combine each pair of consecutive radix-2 DIT (Cooley-Tukey) inverse stages into one fused radix-4 butterfly, keeping intermediates in registers. The butterfly network is unchanged (still the exact inverse of the forward, bit-reversed input -> natural output), but the inverse transform now makes 5 memory passes instead of 10. Inverse twiddles ita=w_{4q}^{-j}, itb=w_{4q}^{-(j+q)}, itc=w_{2q}^{-j} are precomputed per fused stage in plan_new (free). ~9.5% fewer fuel operators.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: The negacyclic pre-weight a_i *= psi^i was a separate pass over the data that materialized the u64 transform buffers. Fold it into the first fused radix-4 forward pass: the first butterfly reads the raw u32 inputs, multiplies by psi on the fly, and writes the u64 buffers directly. Eliminates a full pass (its stores and reloads) per operand per prime. Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: The negacyclic pre-weight a_i *= psi^i was a separate pass over the data that materialized the u64 transform buffers. Fold it into the first fused radix-4 forward pass: the first butterfly reads the raw u32 inputs, multiplies by psi on the fly, and writes the u64 buffers directly. Eliminates a full pass (its stores and reloads) per operand per prime. Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: The negacyclic post-weight (multiply each inverse-NTT output by psi^{-j}*N^{-1}) was a separate pass. Peel the last fused radix-4 inverse pass out of the loop and multiply its four outputs by ipsi before storing, eliminating the separate post-weight pass per prime. Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: The negacyclic post-weight (multiply each inverse-NTT output by psi^{-j}*N^{-1}) was a separate pass. Peel the last fused radix-4 inverse pass out of the loop and multiply its four outputs by ipsi before storing, eliminating the separate post-weight pass per prime. Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: The elementwise spectral product fa[i] *= fb[i] was a separate pass between the forward and inverse transforms. Fold it into the first fused radix-4 inverse pass (q=1), which reads each element exactly once: multiply by fb on load before the butterfly. Eliminates the separate pointwise pass per prime. Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: The elementwise spectral product fa[i] *= fb[i] was a separate pass between the forward and inverse transforms. Fold it into the first fused radix-4 inverse pass (q=1), which reads each element exactly once: multiply by fb on load before the butterfly. Eliminates the separate pointwise pass per prime. Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: In the Garner CRT step, v0 = r0[j] is already a residue mod P0, and P0 < P1, so v0 % P1 == v0. Drop that modulo, computing t1 = (r1[j] + P1 - v0) % P1 directly. Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: In the Garner CRT step, v0 = r0[j] is already a residue mod P0, and P0 < P1, so v0 % P1 == v0. Drop that modulo, computing t1 = (r1[j] + P1 - v0) % P1 directly. Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: In the fused radix-4 forward butterfly, several intermediate difference terms (x0+p-x2, s02+p-s13, etc.) feed only a subsequent '* twiddle % p'. Since those values stay in (0, 2p) and the product (< 2p * p < 2^61) fits in u64, the final % p reduces fully on its own -- the separate conditional subtractions on those terms are redundant. Removing four conditional subtracts per butterfly (in both r4_dif and the pre-weighted first-pass variant) cuts ~12% of fuel. All outputs remain in [0, p); correctness unchanged.
entries.approach: In the fused radix-4 forward butterfly, several intermediate difference terms (x0+p-x2, s02+p-s13, etc.) feed only a subsequent '* twiddle % p'. Since those values stay in (0, 2p) and the product (< 2p * p < 2^61) fits in u64, the final % p reduces fully on its own -- the separate conditional subtractions on those terms are redundant. Removing four conditional subtracts per butterfly (in both r4_dif and the pre-weighted first-pass variant) cuts ~12% of fuel. All outputs remain in [0, p); correctness unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Mirror the forward lazy-reduction change in the inverse DIT butterfly. The terms p2=x2+v3 and p3=x2+p-v3 feed only the multiplies va=p2*wa and vb=p3*wb; since they stay in (0,2p) and the product fits u64, the % p reduces them fully and their conditional subtractions are redundant. Applied across all three inverse sites (first pass with pointwise fold, middle passes, last pass with post-weight). Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: Mirror the forward lazy-reduction change in the inverse DIT butterfly. The terms p2=x2+v3 and p3=x2+p-v3 feed only the multiplies va=p2*wa and vb=p3*wb; since they stay in (0,2p) and the product fits u64, the % p reduces them fully and their conditional subtractions are redundant. Applied across all three inverse sites (first pass with pointwise fold, middle passes, last pass with post-weight). Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: In the radix-4 fused transforms the q=1 pass has ta=tc=1 (forward) and ita=itc=1 (inverse); only tb / itb = w_4^{+-1} are nontrivial. Peel the q=1 forward pass into a specialized butterfly that omits the *ta and *tc multiplies, and specialize the already-peeled q=1 inverse pass to drop the *wc weightings of v1,v3 and the *wa multiply. Removes three modular multiplies per butterfly in those passes. Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: In the radix-4 fused transforms the q=1 pass has ta=tc=1 (forward) and ita=itc=1 (inverse); only tb / itb = w_4^{+-1} are nontrivial. Peel the q=1 forward pass into a specialized butterfly that omits the *ta and *tc multiplies, and specialize the already-peeled q=1 inverse pass to drop the *wc weightings of v1,v3 and the *wa multiply. Removes three modular multiplies per butterfly in those passes. Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: In the Garner step computing w = (v0 + P0*v1) mod P2, the term v0 (< P0 < 2^30) plus (p0_mod_p2 * v1 % P2) (< P2) sums to under 2^31, so the outer % P2 reduces the whole sum correctly without separately reducing v0 first. Drops one modulo per coefficient. Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: In the Garner step computing w = (v0 + P0*v1) mod P2, the term v0 (< P0 < 2^30) plus (p0_mod_p2 * v1 % P2) (< P2) sums to under 2^31, so the outer % P2 reduces the whole sum correctly without separately reducing v0 first. Drops one modulo per coefficient. Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: In the forward radix-4 butterfly, the sum terms u=x0+x2 and v=x1+x3 were each conditionally reduced to [0,p), then combined. But u and v feed only the output i0=(u+v) and i1=(u-v)*tc, both of which end in a % p. Keep u,v lazily in [0,2p) and let the output reductions handle it: i0=(u+v)%p and i1=((u+2p-v)*tc)%p (the +2p keeps the difference non-negative; products stay < 2^63). This removes two conditional subtractions per butterfly across all forward passes (r4_dif, the pre-weighted first pass, and the q=1 pass). ~10% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: In the forward radix-4 butterfly, the sum terms u=x0+x2 and v=x1+x3 were each conditionally reduced to [0,p), then combined. This removes two conditional subtractions per butterfly across all forward passes (r4_dif, the pre-weighted first pass, and the q=1 pass). ~10% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Mirror the forward lazy-reduction change in the inverse DIT butterfly. The terms p0=x0+v1 and p1=x0+p-v1 stay lazily in [0,2p) and feed only outputs that end in a % p. In the middle/first passes the outputs become (p0+va)%p etc.; in the last pass each output already feeds a *ipsi%p post-weight, so o0..o3 stay lazy too and their conditional subtractions vanish entirely. Removes 2-6 conditional subtracts per butterfly across the inverse passes (the +2p guard keeps lazy differences non-negative; all products stay < 2^63). ~11% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.

Removes 2-6 conditional subtracts per butterfly across the inverse passes (the +2p guard keeps lazy differences non-negative; all products stay < 2^63). ~11% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Two cleanups. (1) The remaining conditional subtraction on the forward butterfly output i2 = m02 + m13 is replaced by (m02 + m13) % p in all three forward butterflies (plain %p is cheaper than the cmp+branch+sub under the fuel metric, consistent with earlier measurements). (2) In the Garner CRT, t1 = r1 + p1 - v0 feeds only t1 * inv01 % p1, so it stays lazy in [0, 2*P1) with no separate reduction. Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: Two cleanups. (1) The remaining conditional subtraction on the forward butterfly output i2 = m02 + m13 is replaced by (m02 + m13) % p in all three forward butterflies (plain %p is cheaper than the cmp+branch+sub under the fuel metric, consistent with earlier measurements). (2) In the Garner CRT, t1 = r1 + p1 - v0 feeds only t1 * inv01 % p1, so it stays lazy in [0, 2*P1) with no separate reduction. Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Two more Garner intermediates that feed only a subsequent modular multiply are kept lazy: w = v0 + (p0_mod_p2*v1 % P2) (< 3*P2) and t2 = r2 + 3*P2 - w (< 4*P2, the +3*P2 keeps it non-negative). v2 = (t2 * inv_m01) % P2 reduces fully, so the explicit reductions of w and t2 are unnecessary. Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: Two more Garner intermediates that feed only a subsequent modular multiply are kept lazy: w = v0 + (p0_mod_p2*v1 % P2) (< 3*P2) and t2 = r2 + 3*P2 - w (< 4*P2, the +3*P2 keeps it non-negative). v2 = (t2 * inv_m01) % P2 reduces fully, so the explicit reductions of w and t2 are unnecessary. Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: In the q=1 forward butterfly ta=1, so m02 = x0 + p - x2 is a plain difference (not a product) feeding only output % p reductions. Keep it lazy in (0,2p): i2 = (m02+m13)%p and i3 = (m02+2p-m13)%p (the +2p keeps it non-negative), removing one reduction per q=1 butterfly. Outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: In the q=1 forward butterfly ta=1, so m02 = x0 + p - x2 is a plain difference (not a product) feeding only output % p reductions. Keep it lazy in (0,2p): i2 = (m02+m13)%p and i3 = (m02+2p-m13)%p (the +2p keeps it non-negative), removing one reduction per q=1 butterfly. Outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Regroup the 10 radix-2 forward stages as 3+3+2+2 instead of 2+2+2+2+2: two fused radix-8 passes (the first folding in the psi pre-weight) followed by two fused radix-4 passes. The butterfly network is unchanged, so the output stays in base-2 bit-reversed order and remains compatible with the radix-4 DIT inverse. Beyond cutting one memory pass, the larger radix-8 butterfly exposes more lazily-reducible sum outputs, so the total number of modular reductions drops as well. All intermediate lazy bounds are tracked so products stay < 2^63 (8p*p) and differences stay non-negative. ~6% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: Regroup the 10 radix-2 forward stages as 3+3+2+2 instead of 2+2+2+2+2: two fused radix-8 passes (the first folding in the psi pre-weight) followed by two fused radix-4 passes. The butterfly network is unchanged, so the output stays in base-2 bit-reversed order and remains compatible with the radix-4 DIT inverse. Beyond cutting one memory pass, the larger radix-8 butterfly exposes more lazily-reducible sum outputs, so the total number of modular reductions drops as well. All intermediate lazy bounds are tracked so products stay < 2^63 (8p*p) and differences stay non-negative. ~6% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Mirror the radix-8 forward on the inverse: regroup the 10 radix-2 DIT stages as 2+2+3+3, keeping the first two radix-4 passes (the first folding in the pointwise product) and replacing the last three with two fused radix-8 DIT passes (the last folding in the psi^{-j}*N^{-1} post-weight). Same butterfly network, so it remains the exact inverse of the forward transform; fewer memory passes and more lazily-reducible outputs. Radix-8 twiddle tables are sized [u64;256] (only indices < 256 are used) to keep the precomputed Plan within the wasm meter's linear memory. ~4% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: Mirror the radix-8 forward on the inverse: regroup the 10 radix-2 DIT stages as 2+2+3+3, keeping the first two radix-4 passes (the first folding in the pointwise product) and replacing the last three with two fused radix-8 DIT passes (the last folding in the psi^{-j}*N^{-1} post-weight). Same butterfly network, so it remains the exact inverse of the forward transform; fewer memory passes and more lazily-reducible outputs. Radix-8 twiddle tables are sized [u64;256] (only indices < 256 are used) to keep the precomputed Plan within the wasm meter's linear memory. ~4% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Fuse the forward NTT's last two radix-4 passes (stages L=8,4 and L=2,1) into a single radix-16 pass over each 16-block: load 16 values, run all four radix-2 DIF stages in registers with lazy sums, store back. Forward is now 3 memory passes (r8, r8, r16) instead of 4. The last stages' twiddles are constant 16th-root powers (independent of block position), so no twiddle table is needed. The only multiplies are on differences bounded by < 8p, so products stay < 8p*p < 2^63 -- the 16p-range values appear only in the final, multiply-free stage, so there is no overflow. Same butterfly network => bit-reversed output unchanged; ~5.6% fewer fuel operators.
entries.approach: Fuse the forward NTT's last two radix-4 passes (stages L=8,4 and L=2,1) into a single radix-16 pass over each 16-block: load 16 values, run all four radix-2 DIF stages in registers with lazy sums, store back. Forward is now 3 memory passes (r8, r8, r16) instead of 4. The last stages' twiddles are constant 16th-root powers (independent of block position), so no twiddle table is needed. The only multiplies are on differences bounded by < 8p, so products stay < 8p*p < 2^63 -- the 16p-range values appear only in the final, multiply-free stage, so there is no overflow. Same butterfly network => bit-reversed output unchanged; ~5.6% fewer fuel operators.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: Mirror the radix-16 forward on the inverse: fuse the inverse's first two radix-4 DIT passes (stages L=1,2,4,8, including the pointwise-product fold) into a single radix-16 DIT pass over each 16-block, using constant inverse-16th-root twiddles. Inverse is now 3 memory passes (r16, r8, r8). In DIT only the reduced upper operand is multiplied, so values grow linearly (< 5p) and every product stays < 4p*p < 2^62 (well within u64). Removes the now-unused inverse radix-4 twiddle tables. ~2.8% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.
entries.approach: Mirror the radix-16 forward on the inverse: fuse the inverse's first two radix-4 DIT passes (stages L=1,2,4,8, including the pointwise-product fold) into a single radix-16 DIT pass over each 16-block, using constant inverse-16th-root twiddles. Inverse is now 3 memory passes (r16, r8, r8). In DIT only the reduced upper operand is multiplied, so values grow linearly (< 5p) and every product stays < 4p*p < 2^62 (well within u64). Removes the now-unused inverse radix-4 twiddle tables. ~2.8% fewer fuel operators; outputs unchanged.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note: In both radix-16 passes the first butterfly of every block (kk=0) has twiddle w16^0 = 1. In the forward DIF that difference needs no multiply and stays lazy (no % p); in the inverse DIT the upper operand likewise needs no twiddle multiply, so it stays lazy too (switching the inverse offset to bound-doubling so the lazy difference stays non-negative). This removes ~15 modular reductions per 16-block in each pass. All mult paths still peak below 8p*p < 2^63; outputs unchanged. ~2.6% fewer fuel operators.
entries.approach: In both radix-16 passes the first butterfly of every block (kk=0) has twiddle w16^0 = 1. In the forward DIF that difference needs no multiply and stays lazy (no % p); in the inverse DIT the upper operand likewise needs no twiddle multiply, so it stays lazy too (switching the inverse offset to bound-doubling so the lazy difference stays non-negative). This removes ~15 modular reductions per 16-block in each pass. All mult paths still peak below 8p*p < 2^63; outputs unchanged. ~2.6% fewer fuel operators.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note:
Rewrite the 3-prime negacyclic NTT to be **division-free** under the weighted
cost model (where an i64 `%`/divide costs ~25x an add, as on real hardware).
Every modular multiply by a precomputed constant — the radix-4 twiddles, the
psi negacyclic pre/post weights, and the Garner CRT mixing constants — uses
**Shoup's method**: with `c' = floor(c*2^32/p)` precomputed (free, in plan_new),
`x*c mod p` is `q = (x*c')>>32; r = x*c - q*p; if r>=p {r-p} else {r}` — a couple
of multiplies, a shift and a conditional subtract, but **no division**. Sums are
kept eagerly reduced in [0,p) with a conditional subtract (~2 ops) rather than
deferred to a later `%` (the old lazy-reduction trick, which the weighted metric
correctly penalizes). Radix-4 DIF forward / DIT inverse (1024 = 4^5, five
passes), no bit-reversal. Only the spectral pointwise product (both operands
variable) and the per-input coefficient reduction still use `%`.
entries.approach:
Rewrite the 3-prime negacyclic NTT to be **division-free** under the weighted
cost model (where an i64 `%`/divide costs ~25x an add, as on real hardware).
Every modular multiply by a precomputed constant — the radix-4 twiddles, the
psi negacyclic pre/post weights, and the Garner CRT mixing constants — uses
**Shoup's method**: with `c' = floor(c*2^32/p)` precomputed (free, in plan_new),
`x*c mod p` is `q = (x*c')>>32; r = x*c - q*p; if r>=p {r-p} else {r}` — a couple
of multiplies, a shift and a conditional subtract, but **no division**. Sums are
kept eagerly reduced in [0,p) with a conditional subtract (~2 ops) rather than
deferred to a later `%` (the old lazy-reduction trick, which the weighted metric
correctly penalizes). Radix-4 DIF forward / DIT inverse (1024 = 4^5, five
passes), no bit-reversal. Only the spectral pointwise product (both operands
variable) and the per-input coefficient reduction still use `%`.
entries.iterationNotes:
Local SCORE 34777856 (9.19x vs baseline 319558256), -8.3% vs the prior record
37923024. All 7 correctness tests pass; only src/algorithm/ changed. Remaining
headroom toward the projected ~21M: a register-fused radix-16 (3 memory passes
instead of radix-4's 5) and SIMD (wasm v128 ALU ops are 1 fuel each), both of
which the division-free Shoup kernel is now well-suited to.

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note:
Convert both radix-4 transforms to **Harvey-style lazy reduction**. The
division-free Shoup multiply is split into a *lazy* form `shoup_lazy` that
computes `x*c mod p` but leaves the result in `[0, 2p)` — it omits the final
conditional subtraction entirely. This is valid because, with the 2^32 Shoup
constant `c' = floor(c*2^32/p)`, the quotient estimate is off by at most 1 for
*any* input `x < 2^32` (Harvey, "Faster arithmetic for NTTs"); the butterflies
keep all values `< 4p < 2^32`, so inputs are always in range.

Each butterfly now keeps its lattice of values in `[0, 2p)` (intermediate sums
in `[0, 4p)`), reducing with a single `>= 2p` subtract only where needed, and
every twiddle multiply (and the 4th-root multiply) uses `shoup_lazy` with no
conditional subtract. The net effect is roughly one fewer conditional subtract
per modular multiply plus far fewer sum-reductions, versus the strict-`[0,p)`
eager version of entry 0024 — same exact outputs, fewer operations.

conditional subtraction entirely. This is valid because, with the 2^32 Shoup
constant `c' = floor(c*2^32/p)`, the quotient estimate is off by at most 1 for
*any* input `x < 2^32` (Harvey, "Faster arithmetic for NTTs"); the butterflies
keep all values `< 4p < 2^32`, so inputs are always in range.

Each butterfly now keeps its lattice of values in `[0, 2p)` (intermediate sums

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note:
Eliminate the per-input coefficient reduction. The forward transform previously
did `a[j] % p` before the negacyclic psi pre-weight. But `shoup_lazy` is valid
for any operand `< 2^32`, so the raw u32 coefficient can be fed straight in:
`shoup_lazy(a[j], psi^j, ...)` computes `a[j]*psi^j mod p` in `[0,2p)` directly,
folding the reduction into the multiply that was happening anyway. Removes one
`% p` (the most expensive op, weight 25) per input coefficient across all six
forward transforms — with identical outputs.
entries.approach:
Eliminate the per-input coefficient reduction. The forward transform previously
did `a[j] % p` before the negacyclic psi pre-weight. But `shoup_lazy` is valid
for any operand `< 2^32`, so the raw u32 coefficient can be fed straight in:
`shoup_lazy(a[j], psi^j, ...)` computes `a[j]*psi^j mod p` in `[0,2p)` directly,
folding the reduction into the multiply that was happening anyway. Removes one
`% p` (the most expensive op, weight 25) per input coefficient across all six
forward transforms — with identical outputs.
